steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which statement about the "some researchers" view is best supported. That view says Grande Terre's flora and fauna descended from clades that lived on nearby islands before Grande Terre emerged 37 million years ago. To test the view, check whether each clade's crown age is older (supports the view) or younger (contradicts it) than 37 million years.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'true of stick insects but not flea beetles': The stick insect crown age is 41.1 million years — older than 37 million, so the view holds. The flea beetle crown age is 12.8 million years — younger than 37 million, so the view fails for that group. This matches the evidence exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'weakened by findings of Buckley et al.': Buckley found the stick insect crown age at 41.1 million years, which is older than 37 million and actually supports the view, not weakens it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Grande Terre may have emerged as early as 41.1 million years ago': The 41.1 million year figure is the crown age of the stick insect clade, not a claim about when Grande Terre emerged. The text states Grande Terre emerged 37 million years ago. ✗
  • Choice D — 'depends on inaccurate assumptions about crown ages': The crown ages are empirical findings from named researchers — the text treats them as established data, not as assumptions being questioned. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
